behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at local module handoff (evaluate command -> artifact writer -> artifact store keying). A pure unit test can miss wrong slot/key wiring even if serialization logic is correct. No OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with a realistic issue fixture and an in-memory/local artifact store. Assert a new/updated `grounding` artifact exists with expected content shape, and `plan` is unchanged by this stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is read/write coordination across modules (artifact reader + implement flow + artifact writer). Mocking only one function can hide key mismatch or precedence bugs. No real external infra is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifacts with both `grounding` and preexisting non-plan artifacts, execute implement-stage, and assert: (1) implement input includes grounding-derived context, (2) output is written to `plan`, (3) unrelated artifacts are untouched.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is unintended side effect against artifact storage when conditional logic and write path interact. A unit test of the condition alone can pass while integration still writes `plan` due to wiring/default behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content that already contains diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with local artifact store pre-seeded with a `plan`, and assert deep-dive does not mutate/replace `plan` and does not emit redundant plan write events.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: The target failure spans end-to-end pipeline sequencing and cross-step state propagation through multiple stages. Single-stage integration tests can miss ordering/session-state regressions that appear only in full chained execution.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one end-to-end pipeline run through deep-dive, evaluate, and implement in the real orchestrator path. Assert evaluate emits `grounding`, implement consumes that same run’s grounding, and final `plan` includes grounding-specific facts/constraints not present in the initial issue alone.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback-state handling across artifact lookup, defaulting logic, and write isolation. Mocked happy-path units may miss null/absent artifact interactions and accidental overwrite of sibling artifacts.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage with no `grounding` artifact but with other artifacts present. Assert implement completes, writes syntactically/structurally valid `plan`, records fallback path (if expected), and preserves all non-plan artifacts byte-for-byte.
